title: Chaetospora dactyloides
taxonomy: Chaetospora dactyloides is classified as a species of fungus within the Ascomycota phylum. The specific classification is within the order Pleosporales, family Pyrenulaceae. Chaetospora dactyloides is further classified as a genus within the family Pyrenulaceae, which is a group of fungi. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Pyrenulaceae subfamiliae and the genus itself is placed within the subfamily.

intro: Chaetospora dactyloides, also known as Dactylaria, is a genus of fungi that belongs to the family Chaetosphaeriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Asia, to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Chaetospora dactyloides include polysaccharides, glycoproteins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chaetospora dactyloides include tea, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
